_instructions: Copy the template below and add to the 'questions' array.
id         : Unique integer for this question (increment from last)
options    : Array of exactly 4 answer strings
answer     : Index of the correct option (0=A, 1=B, 2=C, 3=D)
explanation: Detailed explanation of the correct answer

questions.question: Which cranial nerve is responsible for the afferent limb of the corneal reflex?
options: Cranial Nerve V1 (Ophthalmic branch of Trigeminal)
questions.explanation: The afferent limb of the corneal reflex is mediated by the ophthalmic branch (V1) of the trigeminal nerve (CN V). Touching the cornea stimulates free nerve endings that travel via the nasociliary branch of V1 to the trigeminal sensory nucleus. The efferent limb is CN VII (facial nerve) causing orbicularis oculi contraction and eye closure.

questions.question: A patient presents with loss of pain and temperature on the right side of the face and the left side of the body. Which structure is most likely damaged?
options: Right medullary lateral tegmentum (lateral medullary syndrome)
questions.explanation: This crossed sensory pattern (ipsilateral face, contralateral body) is classic for lateral medullary syndrome (Wallenberg syndrome), caused by occlusion of the posterior inferior cerebellar artery (PICA) or vertebral artery. The right spinal trigeminal nucleus (ipsilateral face pain/temp) and the right spinothalamic tract before it has crossed (left body pain/temp) are both damaged in the right lateral medulla.

questions.question: The internal capsule carries corticospinal fibers in which anatomical region?
questions.explanation: Corticospinal (pyramidal) fibers travel in the posterior limb of the internal capsule. The anterior limb contains frontopontine and thalamofrontal fibers. The genu contains corticobulbar fibers (to cranial nerve motor nuclei). The retrolenticular part contains optic radiations and posterior thalamic projections.
